title: BlackSky to accelerate broad-area collection work under NRO contract modification
summary: The National Reconnaissance Office (NRO) modified a contract with BlackSky Technology to speed up the development of AROS broad-area-collection satellites. This initiative aims to enhance satellite capabilities for wide-area surveillance.

title: New Glenn forced an explosive rewrite for NASA’s plans to build a moon base
summary: NASA's lunar base plans were significantly altered due to the development of Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket. The agency's original 'Ignition' event outlined a new direction for human spaceflight.

title: Iceye raises 1 billion euros to expand SAR satellite systems
summary: Iceye, a Finnish company specializing in radar imaging satellites, has secured over 1 billion euros in funding, valuing the company at more than 10 billion euros. The capital will be used to expand its Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) satellite systems.

title: Isar Aerospace raises 270 million euros for global launch expansion
summary: Isar Aerospace secured 270 million euros ($312 million) in funding to expand its global launch capabilities. The European launch company is preparing for its next launch attempt.

title: Commercial launch providers shortlisted for China’s low-cost cargo spacecraft
summary: China has shortlisted four commercial rocket companies to launch a new low-cost cargo spacecraft for its space station. This move expands the role of private firms in the country's space logistics.

title: Let’s procure in-orbit services like Earth observation data and services
summary: Chinese company MizarVision gained attention for open-source Earth observation data analysis over the Middle East, revealing images of F-35s 24 hours before Operation Epic. The news highlights the growing role of in-orbit services like Earth observation data procurement.

title: FCC lets Amazon Leo miss deployment deadline with temporary spectrum penalty
summary: The FCC has granted Amazon's Project Kuiper a temporary reprieve from its deployment deadline for its broadband satellite constellation, but with a spectrum penalty that may disadvantage it against competitors like SpaceX. Amazon can now miss the July 30 deadline for deploying half of its planned 3,232 satellites.

title: Russia is jamming GPS from space
summary: Russia is reportedly jamming GPS signals from space, posing a significant risk to American GPS systems. This development highlights concerns over potential high-impact GPS interference.

title: Quantum Space to go public in SPAC deal
summary: Quantum Space, a company led by a former NASA administrator, will go public by merging with a SPAC. The company focuses on developing highly maneuverable spacecraft for national security missions.

title: WRC-27: the next arena for U.S.-China space competition
summary: China has filed for 200,000 additional satellites, signaling its aggressive push to dominate the space economy. This move sets the stage for competition with the U.S. ahead of the WRC-27 regulatory discussions.

title: Astronaut captures snake-like auroras from a SpaceX Dragon | Space photo of the day for June 9, 2026
summary: A NASA astronaut captured a stunning image of snake-like auroras from a SpaceX Dragon spacecraft while in orbit. The photo was featured as the Space photo of the day for June 9, 2026.

title: Why Steven Spielberg's 'Disclosure Day' feels like the most exciting sci-fi release of the year
summary: Steven Spielberg's upcoming 'Disclosure Day' is generating excitement as a sci-fi film inspired by 'X-Files'-style conspiracy theories. The movie is seen as a refreshing departure from mainstream mega-franchises.

title: How should we handle alien detection in a world of AI, deepfakes and social media? This committee is writing the rulebook
summary: A committee is developing guidelines for handling potential alien detection in an era of AI, deepfakes, and social media. The effort aims to ensure transparency if real extraterrestrial life is discovered.

title: Watch the Jupiter-Venus conjunction unfold live online today
summary: A live online event will showcase the Jupiter-Venus conjunction starting at 3:30 p.m. (1930 GMT) on June 9. This celestial event can be observed through a livestream.

title: Scientists propose spraying chemicals into Earth's magnetic field to protect us from powerful solar storms
summary: Scientists have proposed a spacecraft-based system to spray chemicals into Earth's magnetic field to strengthen it against powerful solar storms. This approach aims to reduce the impact of solar storms on our planet.

title: James Webb Space Telescope weighs 'sleeping giant' black hole from 10 billion light-years away — and it's 6 billion times our sun's mass
summary: Astronomers used the James Webb Space Telescope to measure a dormant supermassive black hole 10 billion light-years away, finding it to be 6 billion times the mass of our sun. Einstein's theories aided in this unprecedented observation.

title: Why do Venus and Jupiter meet up in the sky so often? It's a symptom of a solar system that supports life on Earth
summary: Venus and Jupiter frequently align in the sky due to the solar system's conditions that also support life on Earth. This celestial phenomenon highlights the stability required for habitable environments.

title: This former Paralympic athlete could become the 1st person with a physical disability to live in orbit
summary: John McFall, a former Paralympic athlete, may become the first person with a physical disability to live in orbit aboard the private Haven-1 space station as early as 2027.

title: SpaceX just launched — and landed — a Falcon 9 rocket booster for the 35th time. That's a new record. (video)
summary: SpaceX achieved a new record by launching and landing a Falcon 9 rocket booster for the 35th time. The mission carried 29 Starlink satellites into orbit from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station.

title: NASA Knows: What Is Mass Distribution?
summary: NASA explains the concept of mass distribution, highlighting its importance in various scientific and engineering fields. The article is designed for students in grades 5-8.

title: San Francisco’s Metropolitan Mosaic
summary: The news describes San Francisco's Metropolitan Mosaic, highlighting its urban development, green spaces, and maritime activity in Northern California. No specific space industry news is mentioned.

title: How NASA Science and Artemis Are Shaping the 2026 FIFA World Cup
summary: NASA will host an exhibit at the 2026 FIFA World Cup Fan Festival in Houston, showcasing how space science and the Artemis program benefit life on Earth and future missions. The exhibit aims to engage soccer fans with space exploration and research from the International Space Station.

title: Supersonic!
summary: NASA's experimental X-59 aircraft achieved supersonic flight for the first time on June 5, 2026, with test pilot Jim 'Clue' Less reaching Mach 1.1 during an 81-minute flight from Edwards Air Force Base.

title: NASA’s INCUS Satellites Progress Toward Launch
summary: NASA's INCUS mission, consisting of three satellites, has completed testing at Blue Canyon Technologies in preparation for a launch scheduled in late May 2026. The mission aims to conduct the first space-based survey of tropical convective updraft dynamics.

title: Digging Back in Time in the UAE
summary: Jabal al Fāyah in the UAE, once submerged under a shallow sea, now stands as a desert landmark showcasing the region's ancient watery past and early human adaptation. The geological formation serves as a testament to the UAE's prehistoric environment and human resilience.

title: NASA’s X-59 Aircraft Flies Supersonic for First Time
summary: NASA's X-59 experimental aircraft achieved supersonic flight for the first time, reaching Mach 1.1 during an 81-minute test flight. The milestone paves the way for demonstrating its quiet supersonic capabilities later in 2024.

title: NASA Announces Winners of 2026 University Innovation Competition
summary: NASA announced the Massachusetts Institute of Technology as the first-place winner of the 2026 RASC-AL competition for their lunar power system project. Two other teams from MIT and Virginia Polytechnic Institute also secured second and third places with Mars exploration infrastructure projects.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Moon Mission Research Continues on Earth
summary: NASA's Artemis II Moon Mission research continues on Earth to support safe human exploration of deep space and provide a blueprint for conducting science on the lunar surface. The mission aims to help build a Moon Base and develop a sustainable human presence there.

title: NASA Concludes Antenna Mishap Investigation, Releases Report
summary: NASA has concluded its investigation into the damage to the Deep Space Station 14 (DSS-14) antenna at the Goldstone Deep Space Communications Complex, classifying it as a Type A mishap. The agency has released a report on the incident.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase team has begun cryogenic testing for Booster 20, preparing for the upcoming Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: Launch Preview: Chinese, Rocket Lab, and Starlink flights scheduled
summary: The upcoming week will see launches from China, Rocket Lab, and Starlink, including three Starlink missions. The flights are scheduled from locations in China, California, Virginia, and Florida.

title: NASA concludes MAVEN mission at Mars, uses data to discover new atmospheric phenomenon
summary: NASA has concluded the MAVEN mission at Mars after a six-month effort to regain communication, while also using its data to discover a new atmospheric phenomenon. The mission provided valuable insights into the Martian atmosphere.

title: After New Glenn anomaly, Blue Origin keeps focus on upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions
summary: Blue Origin remains committed to its upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions despite the recent New Glenn anomaly during hotfire testing at Launch Complex 36. The company is focusing on recovery and future objectives.

title: The Exploration Company advances Nyx parachute recovery
summary: The Exploration Company (TEC) has made progress in developing the parachute recovery system for its reusable Nyx spacecraft. This advancement is a key milestone for the spacecraft's reusability and future missions.

title: Space Force evaluating anomaly-related damage, future danger areas
summary: Space Launch Delta 45 is assessing damage and potential future risks after an anomaly occurred. The Eastern Range, operated by SLD 45, has been cleared for operations.

title: Artemis III’s remaining solid rocket booster segments shipped from Utah to Florida
summary: The remaining solid rocket booster segments for Artemis III have been transported from Utah to Florida. This marks a key step in preparation for the mission, despite ongoing uncertainties about timelines and landers.

title: Blue Origin initiates LC-36 recovery, targets RTF by end of 2026
summary: Blue Origin has begun inspection, repair, and reconstruction activities at Launch Complex-36. The company aims to achieve a return-to-flight (RTF) status by the end of 2026.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese rockets to launch internet satellites to LEO
summary: SpaceX and Chinese rockets are scheduled to launch internet satellites to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) this week, with six missions from five organizations featured in the launch manifest.

title: Ancient black hole sheds new light on Webb’s Little Red Dots
summary: An international team used the James Webb Space Telescope to study an ancient black hole, providing new insights into 'Little Red Dots' observed by the telescope. This discovery enhances understanding of early universe phenomena.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing and ensuring materials can withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is supported by these innovative minds.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space missions and education.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry news article titled 'The great parachute bake-out' suggests testing or preparation involving parachutes, likely for a space mission or re-entry system. The specific details or company involved are not provided in the given text.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter diameter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: Reflex Aerospace Taps Arkadia Space for 2027 Mission
summary: Reflex Aerospace has selected Spanish startup Arkadia Space to provide a propulsion system for its satellite mission scheduled for 2027.

title: ICEYE, Isar Aerospace Announce Significant Funding Rounds
summary: ICEYE, a Finnish Earth observation company, raised €450M ($520M) in a Series F funding round at a €10B ($12B) valuation. Isar Aerospace also announced significant funding rounds.

title: Quantum Space To Go Public Via SPAC
summary: Quantum Space announced plans to go public via a SPAC merger to accelerate scaling and access capital. The company emphasized the need for speed in its strategic growth plans.

title: NewOrbit Raises $18.5M to Build VLEO Spacecraft at Scale
summary: NewOrbit has raised $18.5 million to develop Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) spacecraft at scale. The company plans to launch its first VLEO-capable NEO-1 satellite in 2028.

title: Juno Propulsion Raises $1.4M Pre-Seed for RDE Flight
summary: Juno Propulsion secured a $1.4M pre-seed funding round to develop its first flight-ready rotating detonation engine (RDE), named Project Iris, with a demo mission planned for 2027.

title: Mass Is the Easy Part, Coordination Is The Hard Part
summary: Ukraine's use of cheap drones has forced the U.S. to shift its defense strategy toward mass-produced, autonomous, and disposable systems. This marks a significant change in military procurement and technology development.

title: AstroForge Completes its DeepSpace-2 Spacecraft
summary: AstroForge has completed its DeepSpace-2 spacecraft, which aims to demonstrate rendezvous capabilities with an asteroid. This mission sets the foundation for future asteroid mining efforts before the end of the decade.

title: Apex Raises $200M At $2.3B Valuation
summary: Apex has raised $200 million in a funding round, valuing the company at $2.3 billion. This is the third time the company has raised $200 million in approximately 14 months.

title: US Commercial Space Stations Expand to Europe
summary: The U.S. is expanding its commercial space station initiatives to Europe as the ISS nears retirement. This move ensures continued European access to Low Earth Orbit (LEO).

title: Recapping Planet’s Q1 With CFO and President Ashley Johnson
summary: Planet's Q1 recap highlights its differentiation in revenue recognition compared to hardware-focused companies. The discussion emphasizes Planet's approach to handling outcomes in its business model.

title: Quantum Space to Go Public in SPAC Deal
summary: Quantum Space, led by former NASA Administrator Jim Bridenstine, plans to go public via a SPAC merger with Inflection Point Acquisition. This deal will value Quantum Space at a significant amount.

title: Finland’s Voimatel to Integrate Eutelsat LEO for Arctic Communications
summary: Voimatel will integrate Eutelsat's Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) connectivity to enhance Arctic communications coverage. The Finnish company specializes in fixed and wireless communication networks.

title: KSAT to Lead European Pollution-Focused Poseidon EO Mission
summary: KSAT will lead the European Poseidon Earth observation mission aimed at tracking pollution using optical and radar-based satellite technology. The project was announced under a new award.

title: FCC Kicks Off Review of Amazon/Globalstar Deal
summary: The FCC has initiated a review process for Amazon's acquisition of Globalstar, with comments due by July 6. The deal was announced by Amazon in April.

title: Inside the State of the Satellite Industry 2026 Report with SIA President Tom Stroup
summary: The article discusses the State of the Satellite Industry 2026 Report, highlighting projections and demand for satellite broadband and direct-to-device services. It features insights from SIA President Tom Stroup on industry trends and hype.

title: FCC Gives Amazon Leo 50% Deployment Waiver, With Conditions on Spectrum Priority
summary: The FCC granted Amazon a 50% deployment waiver for its Project Kuiper constellation by the end of July, but imposed conditions on spectrum priority. This decision impacts Amazon's satellite internet plans.

title: Apex Hits $2.3B Valuation With Latest Funding Raise
summary: Apex Space, a satellite bus manufacturer, raised an additional $200 million in capital, bringing its valuation to $2.3 billion. This funding round more than doubles the company's previous valuation.

title: Aalyria to Support Orchestration for NASA SCaN Program
summary: NASA has selected Aalyria to support orchestration for its Space Communications and Navigation (SCaN) Program, beginning with the PExT demonstration mission. The PExT mission, known as Polylingual Experimental Terminal, aims to advance communication technologies.

title: Tototheo Global Taps Eutelsat for LEO Service for Maritime
summary: Tototheo Global has partnered with Eutelsat to provide Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite services for maritime applications. The collaboration aims to enhance connectivity and communication solutions in the maritime industry.

title: NGA Awards Planet $22M Luno B Option for Maritime Domain Awareness
summary: The National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) awarded Planet Labs a $22 million option for maritime domain awareness using AI-enabled analytics. This extends a contract initiated last year to support U.S. Combatant Commands.
